content.claim: Finland's 1972-1977 transition from a two-tier grammar/civic system to a comprehensive structure is associated with historical equity gains, but students educated under the comprehensive system did not outperform those in the earlier system until the mid-1990s, approximately two decades after full implementation.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Causal attribution to comprehensive structure alone cannot be separated from simultaneous reforms in teacher training, curriculum design, and welfare state expansion
Initial 1970s-1980s underperformance relative to global average complicates simple causality narrative
Domestic Finnish criticism of the 'levelling effect' was resolved only after PISA 2001 validated the model, suggesting attribution was uncertain even within Finland
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
